title: Learner Profile Schema
description: Parameterizes curriculum generation for a specific learner. Values in this schema are passed directly into generation prompts. LLMs interpret the values semantically — numeric scales and named levels both work; combinations are best. Fork this file and replace values to calibrate for a different learner.

learner.name: Scott Rallya
learner.notes: Self-directed doctoral curriculum; ADHD and bipolar; returning to formal study after hiatus; strong intuition and pattern-recognition, weaker in sustained formal derivation chains; code-native workflow.

cognitive_profile._comment_working_memory: high = reduce symbol density, increase scaffolding; low = can handle dense formalism
cognitive_profile._comment_modality: Order matters — first is strongest preference. Options: narrative, visual-spatial, symbolic, project-based, auditory

formalism_density._comment: 0.0 = pure metaphor/intuition, 1.0 = pure symbolic. 0.4 means ~40% formalism density. Theory.md will still be rigorous, but generation agents lean toward more bridges and examples per symbol block.

example_density._comment: high = multiple worked examples per section, with ML-grounded numbers. low = one per section or fewer.

bridge_density._comment: Number of conceptual bridge paragraphs before each formal definition. high = always 1, sometimes 2 for especially dense sections.

checkpoint_frequency._comment: high = ~5 checkpoints per foundations.md. low = 1-2 per file.

cognitive_profile._comment_exercise: computational = Python/NumPy/SymPy code blocks required. paper = pen-and-pencil derivations. both = offer both. Never use paper-only for this learner profile.

memory_tier_calibration._comment: CARRY is the hard cap on must-memorize items. Exceeding it signals the generation agent to consolidate or promote items to RECONSTRUCT/LOOKUP.

_comment_reading: doctoral = MIT 18.06 / graduate seminar depth. undergraduate = introductory. practitioner = applied, minimal theory.

prior_knowledge._comment: scattered-intermediate = has pieces but no unified framework. expert = can implement from scratch. Values: none, beginner, scattered-intermediate, intermediate, advanced, expert.

_comment_ml: 0.0 = pure abstract math, 1.0 = every concept immediately grounded in ML example. 0.8 means strong ML grounding throughout.

pacing._comment: 1.0 = standard par. >1.0 = allow more time per module. Accounts for deeper engagement and re-reading.

_usage_note: Embed this object in generation prompts as: LEARNER PROFILE: [paste JSON]. The generation agent should treat numeric scores as calibration signals and named levels as semantic instructions. Variables can be overridden per-module in theory.md frontmatter under a learner_overrides key.
